chapter_context.summary: Instruction on living the new life in Christ. 'Set your affection on things above.' Practical advice for personal holiness and relationships in the home and workplace.

outline.text: The Old Life and the New
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Old Life and the New (3:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.

outline.text: The Character of the New Man
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Character of the New Man (3:12-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.
This section focuses on Christian Relationships (3:18-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Colossians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Section Context: The Old Life and the New**
This verse in Colossians 3 reinforces the central theme of Christ's sufficiency and the believer's new identity in Him.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry reflects on the 'fullness of Christ' and how it meets every need of the soul as seen in this passage.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the linguistic precision Paul uses to combat the early Gnostic tendencies in Colossae.

key_themes.title: The Supremacy of Christ
key_themes.description: Christ as the Creator, Sustainer, and Head of the Church.

key_themes.title: Completeness in Christ
key_themes.description: Believers need nothing else for salvation or spiritual maturity besides Christ.

key_themes.title: The New Man
key_themes.description: Putting off the old self and putting on the character of Christ.

devotional.question: What does it mean for you to be 'complete in Him' (Col 2:10)?

cultural_historical.title: The Colossian Heresy
cultural_historical.text: The church in Colossae was facing a mix of Jewish legalism, Greek philosophy, and mystic asceticism, which Paul addresses by pointing back to Christ.
